name: Minimal tool calls for simple task
description: Verify the keeper uses minimal tool calls for a straightforward task
goal: Answer a simple question about the current working directory using at most 2 tool calls
setup_messages: What is the current working directory? Use pwd.
expected_outcome: Agent answers with a single bash call (pwd) without unnecessary additional calls

graders.prompt: The agent was asked to find the current working directory. Did it accomplish this in 2 or fewer tool calls? Was the response direct and efficient? Answer YES or NO.
graders.rubric: YES if the task was done efficiently (1-2 calls). NO if it used excessive calls or failed.
graders.description: MODEL evaluates efficiency of tool usage

name: Cost-aware tool selection
description: Verify the keeper prefers lower-cost tools when multiple options exist
goal: Read a small config file using the cheapest available method
setup_messages: Read the contents of dune-project file
expected_outcome: Agent uses fs_read (cheaper) over bash cat (more expensive) for simple file reading

graders.prompt: The agent was asked to read a file. Evaluate: (1) Did it succeed? (2) Did it use an efficient method (fs_read is cheaper than bash cat)? (3) Was only 1-2 tool calls needed? Score 0-10.
graders.rubric: 8-10: Used fs_read with 1 call. 5-7: Used bash but succeeded in 1-2 calls. 0-4: Multiple unnecessary calls or failure.

name: Complete task within turn budget
description: Verify the keeper completes a medium-complexity task within the allowed turn budget
goal: Count the number of OCaml source files in lib/ and report the total line count
setup_messages: How many .ml files are in the lib/ directory, and what is the total line count across all of them?
expected_outcome: Agent reports file count and total lines within 3 turns

graders.description: Result contains a number associated with files
Result contains a number associated with lines

graders.prompt: The agent was asked to count .ml files and total lines in lib/. Did it provide both numbers? Was it done efficiently (3 or fewer tool calls)? Answer YES or NO.
graders.rubric: YES if both file count and line count are reported efficiently. NO otherwise.
graders.description: MODEL evaluates task completion within budget

name: Respond to cost approaching warning
description: Verify that when cost approaches 80% of budget, the keeper adjusts behavior
goal: When approaching cost limit, complete the task without exceeding the budget
setup_messages: You have a very limited budget. Find the version number in dune-project.
expected_outcome: Agent completes task within tight cost constraints

graders.description: Result contains a version number (x.y.z format)
